The Weekend Hacker

About

Notes on Building a BLE Mesh Prototype

Posted on June 10, 2026 by Aryan K.

Over the past two weekends I've been hacking on a small Bluetooth Low Energy messenger that doesn't need internet at all. The idea is simple: two phones nearby should be able to exchange short text messages directly over BLE, with no server in between.

The trickiest part so far has been getting a stable GATT server running on Android from Kotlin while keeping the actual packet encoding logic in Go, shared via Dart FFI into the Flutter UI layer. There's a lot of ceremony around characteristic notifications that the documentation glosses over.

What's working

Right now I can reliably send a 200-byte payload between two test devices with under a second of latency once the connection is established. Connection setup itself is still slower than I'd like, somewhere around 3-4 seconds on average.

Next up: chunking larger payloads and adding a simple acknowledgement scheme so messages aren't silently dropped when devices briefly move out of range.
